name: Qatar Personal Data Privacy Law (Law No. 13 of 2016)
summary: Qatar's Personal Data Privacy Law establishes comprehensive data protection requirements for organizations processing personal data within Qatar, including the Qatar Financial Centre. It mandates consent-based processing, data minimization, and cross-border transfer restrictions, with enforcement by the Compliance and Data Protection Department.
keyRequirements: Explicit consent required for collection and processing of personal data
Data minimization and purpose limitation principles
Cross-border data transfer restrictions requiring adequate protection or consent
Data processor agreements with mandatory security and confidentiality provisions
Individual rights to access, rectification, and objection to processing
Special protections for sensitive personal data including health, biometric, and genetic data
penalties: Fines up to QAR 5 million for non-compliance. Imprisonment of up to 3 years for serious violations including unauthorized disclosure of personal data. Additional penalties for cross-border transfer violations.
